description_long: This object consists of several stick grenades, commonly known as 'potato masher' grenades, arranged on a flat, dark circular platform. Each grenade features a cylindrical metal head attached to a long, wooden handle, with the heads painted in a muted green color and the handles in a natural wood tone. The grenades are scattered in a loose cluster, with some lying flat and others standing upright, showcasing their distinctive shape and utilitarian design. The metal heads have visible end caps, and the handles appear to have a slight taper and a metal ring or band near the base. The platform beneath the grenades is smooth, round, and dark, providing a stark contrast to the lighter grenades. No visible wires, buttons, or digital components are present, emphasizing a classic, mechanical detonation style.
description: A group of classic stick grenades with green metal heads and wooden handles are scattered on a dark, round platform. The grenades are arranged in various positions, highlighting their distinctive cylindrical heads and long handles.
description_view_0: Several stick grenades are seen from above, with one standing upright and others lying flat on a dark circular base.
description_view_1: The grenades are viewed from a lower angle, revealing more detail on the metal heads and the arrangement of the handles.
description_view_2: This view shows the grenades clustered together, with the upright grenade's handle and head clearly visible.
description_view_3: The grenades are seen from the opposite side, emphasizing the spread of the handles and the metallic end caps.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The grenades appear to use a mechanical detonation mechanism, likely initiated by unscrewing the end cap and pulling a cord or striking the handle to ignite the fuse.
